output: {"match_id": "m000", "winner": "B", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation with well-constructed traps. B's pilot signal on the target dim is materially higher (3.2 vs 2.3), and B's setup is tighter: a single tool response contains every requested field (manager + headcount), with a flat-cost framing and explicit 'do not re-fetch present fields' instruction, making any follow-up call unambiguously redundant. The named redundant-call temptations map cleanly to fields already returned. A is realistic but its 3-call budget is already spent before the trigger turn, shifting the failure mode toward budget-violation rather than pure redundancy, and disciplined models can escape by 'asking before calling' — diluting the signal. B also rolls cleaner paraphrase variations. Higher elicitation + cleaner discrimination favors B."}
